programs.title: Taste the world with Pascal
programs.desc:
Pascal Brodnicki likes to explore the world through his sense of taste.He loves to prepare various dishes at street stalls, in African villages or on the beach. However, during his culinary journeys it was not always easy for him.Sometimes elephants stole his ingredients.Of course, Pascal couldn't deny himself the pleasure of tasting local delicacies.In Delhi, he stopped for a long time at a paratha stand, in a Zanzibar village he ate rice with coconut, and in Namibia he tried a zebra steak."Taste the world with Pascal" is not only about cooking.The host visited an elephant orphanage in Sri Lanka, fed cheetahs in Africa and climbed a coconut tree.In Bombay he became a teacher and taught Polish to children, and on the coast of Zanzibar he hunted octopus.Everywhere his passion and positive energy attracted crowds of onlookers
The next stop on the "Taste the World with Pascal" map is India.Pascal will visit Delhi, where he will taste parathas, traditional wheat pancakes, served with green peas, carrots, bananas or cheese at street stalls.At one of the stalls, Pascal will serve passers-by chicken "Indian style" according to his own recipe.The leader will also visit temples where volunteers prepare free meals for pilgrims.In such kitchens, people cook for up to a thousand people.The cook had never cooked on such a scale.Later, Pascal will go to Bombay, where he will look for the perfect fish for his recipe at the fish market.And then he will prepare it in curry sauce, with peppers and coconut milk.A saleswoman at the local market lends him the kitchen.

programs.title: Shocking dishes
programs.desc: Jarek Nowak is looking for shocking dishes in Peru.He visits Iquitos - a city located on the Amazon River, to which no land route leads.Looking for extreme taste sensations, he reaches the market.There you can buy products straight from the jungle, such as turtles, crocodiles, fish and worms.Traders also sell magical herbs and unusual potions that can cure various ailments.Later, the host pays a visit to a tribe that eats monkeys, turtles, eggs, intestines with their contents, and spicy Karachama fish soup.

programs.title: Shocking dishes
programs.desc: Jarek Nowak goes to Singapore, a city that provides tourists with many delights and surprises.He will look for the strangest dishes and the most interesting delicacies of Asian cuisine.The tasting will start with bird's nest soup, the main ingredient of which is dried swallow saliva, which has extremely health-promoting properties.The locals consider it a real delicacy, and you have to pay dearly for a portion of the soup.Then Jarek will try sheep brain and shark head, before snacking on durian dessert, the smelliest fruit in the world.

programs.title: Indonesia's Best Ever Eats
programs.desc: A culinary journey to the largest island country in the world.Gourmets will discover the best dishes from five regions of Indonesia, including the area around Lake Toba in Sumatra.

programs.title: China according to Jarek Nowak
programs.desc: Jarek Nowak, anthropologist and traveler, goes on an expedition through China to rediscover one of the oldest civilizations in the world.The host will take viewers on a walk through the alleys of the Great Wall of China, tasting street food dishes and trekking through the Leaping Dragon Gorge.There will also be interesting attractions, surprising customs and unusual dishes.The traveler will advise you on how to get around, what to do in your free time and how to experience a once-in-a-lifetime adventure.The program is an alternative guide that contains a lot of interesting facts and practical tips for everyone.

programs.title: Moonshiners 11
programs.desc: Further adventures of producers of illegal alcohol.The demand for their products is growing, which is why this business is becoming more and more dangerous.To expand production, new helpers are needed.
